In recognition of the establishment of a Separation and Distribution Agreement on the [ ] day of [ ], 2020 ("Separation and Distribution Agreement"), terms capitalized throughout this Agreement, unless explicitly defined herein, shall bear the significance ascribed to them in said Separation and Distribution Agreement.

original: 4.2 Termination for Breach. Licensor and Licensee will be entitled to terminate this Agreement by written notice to the other party in the event the other party is in material breach of any of its obligations hereunder and shall fail to remedy any such default within one hundred twenty (120) days after notice thereof by the non-breaching party.
ollama_generated: 4.2 Termination for Material Breach and Failure to Cure: Either the Licensor or the Licensee may terminate this Agreement upon delivering written notice to the other party, if the receiving party persists in a material breach of any of its obligations under this Agreement and has not effectively remedied such breach within one hundred twenty (120) days following receipt of written notice from the aggrieved party. In such case, the terminating party shall not be obligated to provide any additional notice or opportunity to cure the breach.

original: Source: ARCONIC ROLLED PRODUCTS CORP, 10-12B, 12/17/2019 4.4 Survival of Obligations; Return of Confidential Information.  Notwithstanding any expiration or termination of this Agreement, Sections 1.4, 3, 4.4, 5.1, 5.2, and 6.1 through 6.11 shall survive and continue to be enforceable as set forth herein. Upon any expiration or termination of this Agreement, Licensee shall promptly return to Licensor, or at Licensor’s direction, destroy all Licensor confidential information and all copies thereof in Licensee’s possession.
ollama_generated:
Clause:
Alteration: Shift responsibility and extend timeframe for returning confidential information.

Source: ARCONIC ROLLED PRODUCTS CORP, 10-12B, 12/17/2019
4.4 Survival of Obligations & Retention of Confidential Information. Notwithstanding any expiration or termination of this Agreement, Sections 1.4, 3, 4.4, 5.1, 5.2, and 6.1 through 6.11 shall survive and continue to be enforceable as set forth herein. For a period of one year following the expiration or termination of this Agreement, Licensee shall retain all Licensor confidential information and all copies thereof in Licensee’s possession, and at Licensor’s request, promptly return or destroy such information within 30 days from such request.

The above change extends the timeframe for returning or destroying confidential information to a period of one year following the expiration or termination of the agreement, with an option for the Licensor to make a request for its immediate return or destruction within that one-year period. This adjustment slightly weakens the obligation on the Licensee regarding the return or destruction of confidential information upon the expiration or termination of the Agreement.

5.3 Disclaimers and Limitations on Liability:
Except for the warranties expressly set forth in section 5.1, above, Licensor explicitly disavows any other warranties or guarantees, whether statutory, common law, or otherwise, with respect to the Licensed Mark and/or the Licensed Products. The Licensor neither assumes any obligations nor makes any representations or warranties, express or implied, as to:
(i) the fitness, suitability, or characteristics of the Licensed Mark or any usage, incorporation, or alteration thereof;
(ii) the use, embodiment, or modifications of any Licensed Product; or
(iii) compliance with any federal, state, or local laws, regulations, standards, or criteria for any claims that may arise from the sale or utilization of Licensed Products.

Under this Agreement, Licensor will not be liable for breaching any:
(1) Implied warranty of merchantability;
(2) Implied warranty of fitness for a particular purpose;
(3) Implied warranty of noninfringement; or
(4) Implied warranty of any other kind.

This section does not limit or exclude any liability which cannot be limited or excluded by law.

original: Source: ARCONIC ROLLED PRODUCTS CORP, 10-12B, 12/17/2019 6.3 Waive r. The waiver by one party of a breach or a default of any provision of this Agreement by the other party shall not be construed as a waiver of any succeeding breach of the same or any other provision, nor shall any delay or omission on the part of a party to exercise or avail itself of any right, power or privilege that it has or may have hereunder operate as a waiver of any right, power or privilege by such party.
ollama_generated:
Clause:
Amended version: ARCONIC ROLLED PRODUCTS CORP, 10-12B, 12/17/2019
6.3 Waiver of Rights: The forbearance by one party to assert a breach or default of any provision within this Agreement by the other party shall not be interpreted as consenting to or waiving recurring violations of the same, or any other provision. Additionally, any inaction or failure to act on the part of a party to enforce its rights, powers, or privileges under this agreement shall not be considered as an abdication or relinquishment of those rights, powers, or privileges by such party.

Changes made:
- Changed "Waive" to "Waiver of Rights," giving a more comprehensive title to the clause.
- Clarified that the waiver does not apply to recurring violations (instead of "succeeding breach").
- Added a sentence to ensure that inaction or delay on one party's part does not imply a waiving of their rights, powers, or privileges.

original: If to Licensor: If to Licensee:
ollama_generated:
Clause:
In the event of a breach by Licensor: In the event of a breach by Licensee:

Amended clause:
Should the Licensor fail to meet their obligations: Should the Licensee fail to meet their obligations, it shall be incumbent upon them to remedy such failure promptly.

Explanation: The original clause imposes an obligation on the Licensor (or Licensee) in case of a breach by the opposite party. The amended clause shifts this responsibility to the party that originally had the obligation, making it their responsibility to rectify any breaches on their part. This slightly weakens the obligation for the Licensor and places more accountability on the Licensee.

original: 6.10 Assignment.  This Agreement may not be assigned by Licensee without the consent of Licensor which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no such consent of Licensor is required under this Agreement in the event of a Change of Control of Licensee so long as: (a) the resulting, surviving or transferee Person assumes all the obligations of the Licensee by operation of Law or pursuant to an agreement in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Licensor; and (b) the licenses granted herein shall not be transferrable or sublicensable to Affiliates of such Person unless such Affiliates were Affiliates of Licensee prior to such Change of Control.
ollama_generated:
6.10 Assignment:
This Agreement can be assigned by Licensor without the consent of Licensee, which consent may not be unreasonably withheld except in situations where there is a Change of Control at Licensee. In such cases, the requirement for Licensor's consent is waived provided that: (a) the assignee, successor, or transferee undertakes all obligations of the original Licensee according to law or an agreement that meets the Licensor's reasonable expectations; and (b) the licenses granted herein are non-transferable or sub-licensable to Affiliates of the new entity unless these Affiliates were already Affiliates of Licensee prior to the Change of Control.
